Off Calendar Mar-13-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT AND CROSS-COMPLAINANT CENTURYLINK COMMUNICATIONS, LLC'S MOTION TO SEAL EXHIBIT E TO REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IN SUPPORT OF DEMURRER TO
SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT

Appearances:
Eduardo Santacana and Harris Mateen of Cooley LLP (415.693.2000, esantacan@cooley.com, hmateen@cooley.com) for defendant/cross-complainant and moving party;
Patrick Hammon and Andrew Parkhurst of Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP (650.233.4500, patrick.hammon@pillsburylaw.com, andrew.parkhurst@pillsburylaw.com) for plaintiff/cross-defendant and responding party)

This matter is not argued. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Centurylink Communications, LLC, sued as Lumen Technologies Group,'s unopposed motion to seal is GRANTED.

Lumen moves to seal Exhibit E to its Request for Judicial Notice in support of its Demurrer to Plaintiff Inflect, Inc.'s Second Amended Complaint. Exhibit E is a service order between Luman and Solana Foundation. Lumen's evidence establishes that the document is maintained confidentially and includes proprietary commercial information. The court concludes that disclosure would subject Lumen to commercial harm. The court finds that (1) there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record; (2) the overriding interest supports sealing the record; (3) a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; (4) the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and (5) no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest.

The court observes that Exhibit F, the Server Inventory Reduction Agreement, was also conditionally lodged under seal. No party has filed a motion to seal it permanently within the allotted time. It therefore must be unsealed. (See Rules of Court, Rule 2.551, subd. (b)(3)(B).) Lumen is ordered to file an unredacted version of the request for judicial notice that includes Exhibit F in the public filing by March 27, 2026.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Deborah Fuqua, CSR 12948 (T: 415.810.7170, E: dfuquaCSR@gmail.com) (301/CVA)
